title: Authors and Citation
text: Hodge D (2026). ggblanket: Publication-Quality 'ggplot2' Visualisation. R package version 20.0.0, https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggblanket/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ggblanket: Publication-Quality 'ggplot2' Visualisation},   author = {David Hodge},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 20.0.0},   url = {https://davidhodge931.github.io/ggblanket/}, }

title: Publication-Quality ggplot2 Visualisation
text: ggblanket package ggplot2 wrapper functions publication-quality visualisation. seeks support well-designed visualisation, aligning much possible ggplot2 tidyverse conventions.

title: Publication-Quality ggplot2 Visualisation
text: Install CRAN, development version GitHub.
code: install.packages("ggblanket")  pak::pak("davidhodge931/ggblanket")

title: Publication-Quality ggplot2 Visualisation
code: library(ggblanket2) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  set_blanket()  iris |>   gg_point(     x = Sepal.Width,     y = Sepal.Length,     fill = Species,     shape = Species,   ) ggplot2::economics_long |>   gg_area(     x = date,     y = value01,     facet_wrap = variable,   )

title: Publication-Quality ggplot2 Visualisation
text: Use gg_*() wrapper functions : Plot ggplot geom layer single function Map aesthetic facet variables bare names Get colour colour_* arguments inherited fill fill_* arguments Change scale arguments easily prefixed arguments lower x/y scale limit zero, get default scale expansion ggplot2::expansion(c(0, 0.5)) Annotate outside panel easier coord_clip = "" default Access arguments within relevant geom_* function via ... Get global set_blanket() setting defaults. Use global setup function, set_blanket(), : Adjust global theme Adjust global option themes refined based plot scale types etc Adjust global theme fill, colour, linewidth, shape, linetype, size, stroke Adjust global theme fill_palette, colour_palette, shape_palette linetype_palette Adjust global option colour_border, function transform colour colour_palette input fill fill_palette respectively Adjust global option fill_border, function transform fill fill_palette input colour colour_palette respectively

title: Publication-Quality ggplot2 Visualisation

Defaults NULL, uses \(x) ggplot2::ggplot(x).

title: Boxplot ggplot — gg_boxplot
code: ggplot2::diamonds |>   gg_boxplot(     x = color,     y = carat,     y_limits = c(0, NA),   ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.   ggplot2::diamonds |>   gg_boxplot(     x = color,     y = carat,     fill = cut,     y_limits = c(0, NA),   ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.   ggplot2::diamonds |>   dplyr::filter(cut %in% c("Premium", "Ideal")) |>   gg_boxplot(     x = color,     y = carat,     facet_wrap = ggplot2::vars(cut),     y_limits = c(0, NA),   ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

title: Violin ggplot — gg_violin
code: ggplot2::diamonds |>   gg_violin(     x = color,     y = carat,     y_limits = c(0, NA),   ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.   ggplot2::diamonds |>   gg_violin(     x = color,     y = carat,     fill = cut,     y_limits = c(0, NA),   ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.   ggplot2::diamonds |>   dplyr::filter(cut %in% c("Premium", "Ideal")) |>   gg_violin(     x = color,     y = carat,     facet_wrap = ggplot2::vars(cut),     y_limits = c(0, NA),   ) #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

Set global option coord_clip.
